title: Catamaran Charter Wedding in Marbella: Costs & Tips 2026
html:
A catamaran charter wedding in Marbella turns a flat, stable deck off the Costa del Sol into a ceremony space, with most full-day sailing celebrations departing Puerto Banús and priced from roughly EUR 1,400 to EUR 4,000 per day. BoatHire24 lists crewed catamarans suited to small wedding parties along this coast.

Where to set off in Marbella

Marbella sits on Spain's Costa del Sol, and a wedding charter can leave from several genuine departure points within easy reach. Each marina has its own character, so the choice shapes the feel of the day before the boat even casts off.

  • Puerto José Banderas, the glamorous marina in Puerto Banús, is the most common base for catamaran weddings.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, the central town harbour, sits close to the old quarter.
  • Puerto de Cabopino, a smaller marina to the east, suits quieter, more intimate parties.
  • La Bajadilla, Marbella's working port, hosts a number of larger charter vessels.
  • Estepona Marina and the bay off Puente Romano are within a short cruise for ceremonies under sail.

Which catamaran or boat should you rent for a Marbella wedding?

Choose a crewed catamaran for a stable deck and shaded saloon, or a flybridge motor yacht when you want speed and an upper-deck ceremony spot. Capacity and the number of guests usually decide the class more than anything else, so count heads before you count features.

  1. Sailing catamaran: best for relaxed daytime ceremonies, seats around 10 to 12 guests, from EUR 1,400 per day.
  2. Power catamaran: best for steady cruising with little heel, holds up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,800 per day.
  3. Flybridge motor yacht: best for a fast, glamorous arrival, and you can compare this motor yacht such as the Sunseeker Predator 72 for larger parties of up to 12, from EUR 3,000 per day.
  4. Open day boat: best for a short legal-formalities-then-toast outing, suits 6 to 8 guests, from EUR 600 per day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht: best for an evening reception at anchor, hosts up to 12 guests, from EUR 4,000 per day.

How much does a catamaran wedding charter cost in Marbella?

Prices on the Costa del Sol move with season, boat size and whether catering is added, but the bands below give a realistic starting point in euros for 2026.

  • A shared sunset group tour from Puerto Banús costs from EUR 35 per person.
  • A small motorboat half-day for the couple and witnesses costs from EUR 350.
  • A catamaran full-day wedding charter costs from EUR 1,400 in the euro currency.
  • A crewed luxury yacht for a full reception day costs from EUR 4,000.

For a like-for-like view of what is available on your date, you can compare verified boat listings before you commit a deposit, which keeps the budget honest. Catering, flowers and a celebrant are usually arranged separately and added to the base hire.

When is the best season for a Marbella wedding charter?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled weather makes a deck ceremony reliable. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and cost less, though afternoon breezes can build along the coast.

Along this stretch of the Costa del Sol the easterly Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon and raise a short chop, so many couples book a morning or sunset slot for the calmest water and the softest light.

Spain treats commercial wedding charters as skippered hire, so a professional crew runs the boat and no guest licence is needed. The operator confirms the exact requirements, insurance and guest limits for your specific vessel and date.

How does booking a wedding charter work?

You pick a date and boat, confirm the guest count and timing, then pay a deposit to hold the crew and slip. A planner or the skipper will usually walk you through catering, music and the ceremony layout once the boat is reserved.

Reading the this motor yacht page and others like it shows the deck plan, crew and inclusions, which matters when you are placing chairs and a small aisle on board. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 1,400 to EUR 4,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: typically a 4 to 8 hour charter
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: EUR
  • Main departure: Puerto Banús, Marbella

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can you legally get married on a boat in Marbella?

A symbolic ceremony on board is straightforward and popular, while the binding legal paperwork is usually completed on land beforehand. The operator and a local planner confirm what is possible for your chosen date and vessel.

How many guests fit on a wedding catamaran?

Most catamarans in Marbella carry up to 12 guests for a comfortable day charter, which suits an intimate wedding party. Larger receptions are arranged across two boats or moved onto a bigger crewed yacht.

How far does a wedding charter sail?

A typical Marbella celebration stays within about 10 nautical miles of the coast, cruising the bay toward Puente Romano or out past Puerto Banús. This keeps the water sheltered and the journey short for guests.

What is included in the price?

The base hire covers the boat, fuel and a professional crew for the agreed hours. Catering, decoration, a celebrant and any extra hours are quoted separately so you can shape the day to your budget.

Lock in your date early, since the best Marbella catamarans book out fast across the 2026 summer wedding season, and if you own a suitable boat on the Costa del Sol you can list a boat for free and earn from charters between celebrations.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
